# Bilateral Filter

URL: https://img2num.dev/docs/internal/core/internal-code/webgpu/implementations/bilateral-filter

### Files

- `bilateral_filter_rgb.wgsl`
- `bilateral_filter_lab.wgsl`

## Overview

A standard Gaussian blur blurs everything, destroying sharp edges. A Bilateral Filter is an "edge-preserving blur". It looks at two weights for every neighbor pixel:

1. **Spatial Weight ( w s w_s w s ):** How far away is the neighbor? (Closer = higher weight).
2. **Range Weight ( w r w_r w r ):** How similar is the color? (Similar color = higher weight).
If a neighboring pixel is close physically but a completely different color (i.e., it is across an edge), the range weight drops to nearly 0, preventing the colors from bleeding together.

### Weight Formula

w = exp ⁡ ( − d i s t S q 2 σ s p a t i a l 2 ) × exp ⁡ ( − r a n g e S q 2 σ r a n g e 2 ) w = \exp\left(-\frac{distSq}{2\sigma_{spatial}^2}\right) \times \exp\left(-\frac{rangeSq}{2\sigma_{range}^2}\right) w= exp( − 2 σ s p a t ia l 2d i s tS q )× exp( − 2 σ r an g e 2r an g e S q )

:::warning Performance Warning The kernel radius is dynamically determined by `i32(3.0 * params.sigmaSpatial)` . A larger spatial sigma results in an exponentially larger O ( N 2 ) O(N^2) O ( N 2 ) nested loop per pixel. :::
